---
title: "Feature Catalogue"
subtitle: "Read the feature inventory and choose an ID with its inputs, output, and requirements in view."
sidebar: concepts
---

## What the catalogue is for

The feature catalogue is the package's live inventory of extractors—the
functions and models that compute features. Use it when you know the kind of
measurement you want but need its exact feature ID, accepted input, return
type, default settings, or dependency requirements.

The [complete feature catalog](../tour/index.qmd) adds a short scientific
meaning, extraction pattern, fallback warning, and method reference for every
public feature ID. The executable gallery in [Inspect and plot feature
types](../cookbook/feature-type-gallery.qmd) shows representative output
shapes and plots.

## How to read one catalogue entry

| Field | Plain-language meaning |
|---|---|
| `feature_id` | The stable name to pass to `plan_features()` or `extract_features()` |
| `label` | A human-readable name |
| `modalities` | The kinds of input the extractor can consume |
| `output_schema` | Whether the result is a dense feature, an event series, a track series, or another declared output |
| `default_params` | The settings used unless you override them |
| `requires` | Extra packages, models, programs, or services the feature may need |
| `dependency_class` | The broad kind of dependency involved |
| `cost_class` | How the planner classifies the method's compute or dependency cost |
| `requires_opt_in` | Whether the default planning budget excludes the feature |
| `is_public` | Whether the ID belongs to the stable user-facing catalogue |

For example, inspect the MFCC entry before changing its number of coefficients
or time step:

```{python}
from natural_features.workflows import available_features

mfcc = next(
    entry
    for entry in available_features(budget="all")
    if entry.feature_id == "audio.mfcc"
)

{
    "input": mfcc.modalities,
    "returns": mfcc.output_schema,
    "defaults": mfcc.default_params,
    "requires_opt_in": mfcc.requires_opt_in,
}
```

## Public IDs and internal IDs

`available_features()` shows public IDs by default. These are the names the
project promises to keep stable within the public API version. Registered
internal and legacy aliases remain available for reproducing an older recipe
or testing a lower-level implementation, but they appear only with
`public_only=False`. They do not carry the same stability promise as public
IDs.

Public does not mean “installed locally” or “cheap to run.” It means the name
and documented behavior are part of the user-facing API. The dependency and
cost fields answer the separate question of what the method needs in order to
run.

## Planning budgets filter the list

The `budget` argument controls which features appear:

| Budget | Entries included |
|---|---|
| `default` | Features that do not require explicit opt-in |
| `allow_python` | Default features plus optional Python-backed features, but not placeholders |
| `all` | Every registered cost class; placeholders still require `include_placeholders=True` |

The budget is permission to consider a feature. It does not install its
dependencies or download a model.

`as_dataframe=True` returns the same entries as a pandas table. Unknown budget
names are rejected, and placeholder entries remain hidden unless they are
explicitly requested. A placeholder reserves a catalogue name for a feature
that is not presented as a normal runnable method.

## Current feature families

The cards below are generated from the live registry when this page renders.
The prefix before the first dot groups related feature IDs.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
from collections import defaultdict
from natural_features.workflows import available_features

groups = defaultdict(list)
for entry in available_features(budget="allow_python"):
    family = entry.feature_id.split(".", 1)[0]
    groups[family].append(entry)

print("::: {.catalogue-grid}")
for family in sorted(groups):
    entries = sorted(groups[family], key=lambda item: item.feature_id)
    feature_ids = ", ".join(f"`{entry.feature_id}`" for entry in entries[:8])
    if len(entries) > 8:
        feature_ids += f", +{len(entries) - 8} more"
    opt_in = sum(1 for entry in entries if entry.requires_opt_in)
    print(f"""
::: {{.catalogue-card}}
#### {family}
{len(entries)} feature IDs · {opt_in} require opt-in

{feature_ids}
:::
""")
print(":::")
```

The catalogue describes and filters extractors. It does not run them. Use
`plan_features()` to see the chosen extractor, input connection, and parameters
for a particular request, then use `extract_features()` to execute it.

## See also

[Complete feature catalog](../tour/index.qmd) · [Catalogue and planning](catalog-and-planning.qmd) · [Execution modes](execution-modes.qmd)
